items.final_wording: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.

source.title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE infectious-disease, allergy, travel, and referral adaptation
source.population: People with an insect bite or sting, including tick, exotic-insect, local reaction, infection, or systemic-reaction context.
source.setting: Community and primary-care assessment, reassessment, and clinician-led referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting bite or sting type, local inflammatory or allergic features, infection and systemic-reaction assessment, worsening, severe pain, immunocompromise, travel, and sensitive-site context. It does not generate treatment, antibiotics, antihistamines, referral, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.

items.rationale: The exact assessment section supports bite or sting type, timing, local redness, itch, inflammatory or allergic features, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and severity without assigning a diagnosis.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.
